title: struct in6_addr fallback definition may conflict with system headers
description:
Location: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/ipv6_addr.h:43-65 (struct in6_addr detection and fallback)

Risk: The detection logic checks various header guards (IN6ADDR_ANY_INIT, _NETINET_IN_H, etc.) to determine if struct in6_addr is already defined. If a new platform uses a different guard or includes the system header after ipv6_addr.h, the fallback definition at line 62-64 conflicts.

The comment acknowledges this: If this conflicts with your platform struct in6_addr, define LICHEN_HAVE_IN6_ADDR=1 before including this header.

Pattern generating user issue reports: New platform port includes system header after ipv6_addr.h, gets struct redefinition error.

Suggested fix: The current approach (checking common guards + user override) is reasonable, but could be improved by:
1. Using a typedef to a uniquely-named struct internally: struct lichen_in6_addr { uint8_t s6_addr[16]; }; typedef struct lichen_in6_addr lichen_in6_addr_t;
2. Then conditionally typedef struct in6_addr lichen_in6_addr_t or define lichen_in6_addr_t as the custom struct
3. All internal code uses lichen_in6_addr_t

This eliminates the possibility of struct redefinition.
